refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms for teleoperation data streams.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to reduce effective packet loss.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control loop.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms for teleoperation data streams.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication adaptation and teleoperation control logic.
Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality management within teleoperation data streams, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that adaptive algorithms may introduce latency affecting operator control.
Assumes availability of sufficient processing resources in teleoperation module.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integration of real-time monitoring (DP3) in next decomposition level to enhance feedback control.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and data processing.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional communication channel for teleoperation data streams subject to packet loss.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Input of system status and network quality metrics to support adaptive algorithms.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control signals sent to vehicle modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope; low containment of teleoperation-specific logic.
Physical vehicle module; minimal containment of teleoperation communication logic.
High containment of teleoperation communication logic and adaptive algorithms.
Safety monitoring role; limited containment of teleoperation communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Teleoperation module is the best architectural boundary to contain adaptive communication algorithms ensuring packet loss limits.
containment_risks: Potential diffusion if adaptive algorithms require data from Safety Services or NGHE Solution modules.
Risk of interface complexity with Network Link and Telemetry if not well defined.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and monitor cross-module data dependencies to mitigate diffusion risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (adaptive bitrate and error correction) directly addresses the refined FR of maintaining packet loss within limits.

diffusion_risks: Possible data sharing with Safety Services for safety-critical monitoring.
Dependency on Network Link interface quality and telemetry data accuracy.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and minimal diffusion, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and network link monitoring is feasible within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication quality management to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Plan next level decomposition to include real-time monitoring and feedback control (FR2 and FR3).
Clarify interface definitions with Safety Services to manage safety-critical data dependencies.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, further refinement is needed to explicitly include real-time monitoring and feedback control sub-FRs to fully address packet loss management.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

revise.why: To explicitly decompose the communication quality requirement into monitoring, adaptation, and reporting sub-functions for better traceability and design.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module has sufficient processing capability.
open_risks: Latency introduced by adaptive algorithms may affect operator control.
Interface complexity with Safety Services and Network Link.
evidence_needed: Performance data on adaptive bitrate and error correction effectiveness.
Verification results of packet loss under customer-defined network conditions.
